Home Guides

How to Choose a CRM for Your Startup

A CRM (customer relationship management) platform helps startups track leads, manage deals, and retain customers. The right choice depends on your team size, sales process complexity, and budget. Here we break down the key factors to help you decide which CRM fits your needs without overpaying for features you won't use in the first year of operations.

Key Features to Evaluate

Start with contact management, pipeline visualization, and email integration. These three features cover 80% of what early-stage startups need. Avoid platforms that require you to purchase add-on modules for basic reporting.

Top CRM Options for Startups

HubSpot CRM

HubSpot offers a generous free tier with contact management, deal tracking, and email templates. It scales well but gets expensive once you need marketing automation or custom reporting beyond the basic dashboard.

Pipedrive

Pipedrive focuses on pipeline management with a clean visual interface. It is popular with sales-driven startups that want simplicity over feature breadth. Plans start at $14 per user per month.

Attio

Attio is a newer CRM built for modern workflows. It offers flexible data modeling and strong integrations with tools like Slack and Linear. Good for startups that want a CRM that adapts to their process rather than forcing a predefined workflow.

Integration Considerations

Your CRM should connect to your email provider, calendar, and support desk. Native integrations are more reliable than Zapier workarounds. Check whether the CRM offers an open API if you plan to build custom automations later.

Pricing Traps to Avoid

Some CRMs advertise low per-seat prices but charge extra for essential features like custom fields, workflow automation, or removing their branding from outbound emails. Always calculate the total cost for your expected team size at 12 months.

Our Recommendation

If you have fewer than 10 people and a straightforward sales process, start with HubSpot free. If your sales motion is more complex or you want better pipeline control, Pipedrive is the better starting point. Evaluate Attio if flexibility and modern integrations matter more than a large ecosystem of third-party apps.
